prompt: I have a drive-manager skill that routes files from my Desktop Inbox to the right folder. Sometimes it routes wrong because it doesn't have enough context. I want to design the meta-process for: when a routing rule turns out wrong, how do I capture that learning and feed it back into the skill's rules? Spec this so I can build it as a Python script.
assertions: Spec file written to the expected output path
Trigger condition for the feedback loop is explicit (e.g. user moves file post-routing, or user flags mis-route)
Output describes an updated routing rules artifact (file, table, or PR), not a re-routed file
Procedure has at least one decision point that distinguishes 'flaky route, no rule change needed' from 'systemic miss, rule should change'
Mermaid block has at least 5 step nodes plus at least one GATE decision node
Edge Cases include conflicting signals (two corrections disagree) or equivalent
Build handoff names Python-specific capture mechanisms (decorators, context managers, structured logging)
Decision rules are testable on input alone
Session notes describe each of the 8 phases
